'''Kieran Dixon''' (born 22 August 1992) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|fb}} for [[Salford RLFC]]. He has played for the [[England Knights]] at international level.

He has played for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]] and in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and on loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[Hemel Stags]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]]. He also played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the Super League.<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Dixon was born in [[Hackney, London]], England. Dixon grew up in Hemel Hempstead and attended The Cavendish School. He is of Antiguan descent.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/west-indies-rugby-league-team|title=How the West Indies could line-up if their rugby league team returned|date=10 January 2023|website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===London Broncos===
Kieran Dixon made his début for the [[London Broncos]] in the 24-34 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]] match at [[Twickenham Stoop]] on 4 February 2012.<ref name="Profile at londonbroncosrl.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/rugby/fixturesandresults/squad_first_xiii.php?player=105135&includeref=dynamic|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120219100916/http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/rugby/fixturesandresults/squad_first_xiii.php?player=105135&includeref=dynamic|url-status=dead|archive-date=2012-02-19|title=Profile at londonbroncosrl.com|publisher=londonbroncosrl.com|date=2011-12-31|accessdate=2012-01-01}}</ref>

He scored his first try against [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] at the [[Twickenham Stoop]] in a 36-0 win.

He suffered a career threatening injury whilst on international duty in 2013, and returned to first team action towards the end of the [[2014 Super League season]], with the Broncos relegation from the top flight long since confirmed.{{cn|date=March 2023}}

===Hemel Stags===
Dixon was loaned to the [[Hemel Stags]] in a bid to regain fitness on his return to first team with the [[London Broncos|Broncos]].{{cn|date=March 2023}}

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
After a serious injury ruled him out for most of the 2014 season, Dixon joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in 2015. He appeared for Rovers in the [[2015 Challenge Cup|2015 Challenge Cup final]], however his performance in the match was heavily criticised as the team suffered a record-breaking 50-0 defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.dailystar.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-rally-around-kieran-17231414|title=Hull KR to rally around Kieran Dixon after Wembley disaster|first=Julie|last=Stott|date=30 August 2015|website=Daily Star}}</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20150903013022/http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-KR-Kieran-Dixon-bounce-Wembley-horror-8211/story-27709723-detail/story.html Hull KR: Kieran Dixon will bounce back from Wembley horror show – Craig Murdock]</ref> The following week, Dixon bounced back to score two tries in a man-of-the-match display as Hull Kingston Rovers beat Wakefield Trinity.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160314042013/http://asia.eurosport.com/rugby-league/chris-chester-praises-kieran-dixon-for-recovering-from-his-wembley-nightmare_sto4898951/story.shtml Chris Chester praises Kieran Dixon for recovering from his Wembley nightmare]</ref> And on 12th 09-15 went 2 better with yet another man of the match performance and 4 tries against [[Bradford Bulls]] in a match that made sure Rovers secured Super league status for the next season.<ref>{{Cite web|title = Hull KR v Bradford Bulls: Super 8s Qualifiers preview|url = http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/hull-k-r-vs-bradford/preview/44271|website = Sky Sports|access-date = 2016-02-13}}</ref>
[[File:Kieran Dixon.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Dixon playing for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in 2016]]

===Newcastle Thunder===
Dixon played one game on loan for the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in [[RFL League 1]], scoring a try against the [[London Skolars]] in May 2016.{{cn|date=March 2023}}

===Return to London===
Dixon re-signed for the London Broncos after [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] were relegated from the [[Super League]], taking up the offer to return to his home club for the 2017 and 2018 seasons.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/kieran-dixon-looking-forward-to-returning-home/ |title=London Broncos Rugby League {{!}} #ProudToBeBroncos {{!}} » Kieran Dixon looking forward to returning home |website=www.londonbroncosrl.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161107020441/http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/kieran-dixon-looking-forward-to-returning-home/ |archive-date=2016-11-07}} </ref>
[[File:Kieran Dixon London Broncos.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Dixon playing for the London Broncos in 2017]]
Dixon was the top try scorer for the club in 2018, with 26 tries to his name. The club was promoted back to Super League after victory in the "Million Pound game" away in Toronto.

In 2019 London were relegated from the Super League on points difference, with Dixon topping the goal and point scoring record for the Broncos for their 2019 season.
[[File:Kieran Dixon 2019.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Dixon playing for the London Broncos in 2019]]
Dixon again topped the goal and point scoring record for London, in their shortened season that was affected by the global pandemic.

===York City Knights===
On 17 November 2020 it was announced that Dixon would join the [[York City Knights]] from the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/57387/kieran-dixon-joins-york-city-knights|title=KIERAN DIXON JOINS YORK CITY KNIGHTS|website=www.rugby-league.com|accessdate=18 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings===
On 23 June 2022 it was announced that both he and [[Luis Roberts]] would join the [[Widnes Vikings]] on loan for the remainder of the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/widnes-kieran-dixon-luis-roberts-leigh|title=Widnes sign pair on loan from Leigh|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=23 June 2022 |accessdate=27 Jun 2022}}</ref> In October 2022 Dixon made his move permanent with a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/kieran-dixon-makes-permanent-switch-to-the-vikings/ |title=Kieran Dixon makes permanent switch to the Vikings |website=Widnes Vikings |date=3 October 2022 |access-date=31 October 2022}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 10 July 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL League 1]] on a 18-month deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Oldham RLFC|date=10 Jul 2024|title=Oldham swoop for former Super League flyer Dixon|url=https://roughyeds.co.uk/2024/07/oldham-swoop-for-former-super-league-flyer-dixon/}}</ref>

On 11 May 2026 the club confirmed that he had left by mutual consent<ref>{{cite web|website=Oldham RLFC|date=11 May 2026|title=Oldham agree Dixon release|accessdate=12 May 2026|url=https://roughyeds.co.uk/news/oldham-agree-dixon-release/}}</ref>

===Salford RLFC (loan)===
On 10 April 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on short-term loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Apr 2026|accessdate=11 Apr 2026|author=Aaron Bower|title=Kieran Dixon among five to make Salford RLFC switch as Oldham exodus continues|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/kieran-dixon-among-five-to-make-salford-rlfc-switch-as-oldham-exodus-continues}}</ref>

===Salford RLFC (re-join)===
On 15 May 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=15 May 2026|accessdate=16 May 2026|author=Callum Walker|title=Kieran Dixon finds new club following Oldham exit|url=https://www.totalrl.com/kieran-dixon-finds-new-club-following-oldham-exit/}}</ref>

==International==
Dixon played at representative level with the [[England Knights]].

He sustained a career threatening injury in 2013 against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]].
